seed_instruction: Create an email to a professor introducing yourself and your goals.
org_constraint: Begin the first paragraph with the phrase "I hope this email finds you well."
new_constraint: Begin the first paragraph with a question rather than a greeting.
instruction: Create an email to a professor introducing yourself and your goals. Begin the first paragraph with the phrase "I hope this email finds you well." Begin the first paragraph with a question rather than a greeting.
response:
Subject: Introduction and Research Interests - [Your Name]

Dear Professor [Professor's Last Name],

Have you had a moment to review the recent developments in [mention a specific topic, e.g., sustainable urban planning/quantum computing]? I hope this email finds you well. My name is [Your Name], and I am a [Year, e.g., Junior/Master's student] majoring in [Your Major] at [University Name]. I have been following your work on [mention a specific paper, project, or class of theirs] and found your insights on [specific detail] particularly compelling.

My primary academic goal is to [mention your main goal, e.g., pursue a career in clinical research / develop a deeper understanding of historical linguistics]. I am especially interested in exploring how [specific sub-topic] impacts [broader field]. Because of your expertise in this area, I am eager to learn more about your perspective on [specific question or research area].

I would appreciate the opportunity to briefly discuss your work or any potential opportunities to [mention a specific action, e.g., join your lab / attend your office hours / work on a project] under your guidance. Please let me know if you have any availability for a brief meeting or a few minutes to chat during your office hours.

Thank you for your time and for all you contribute to the department.

Best regards,

[Your Name]
[Your Student ID Number, if applicable]
[Your Phone Number/LinkedIn Profile]<turn|>
